item.title: Clean up cmd_run: extract auto-review and trim verbosity
item.intent: cmd_run is leaner and reads top-to-bottom. The inline auto-review block, verbose bash idioms in the sprint loop, and nested helper definitions are cleaned up. Saves ~65 lines.
item.description: cmd_run (478 lines) has: (1) a 68-line auto-review block at the end duplicating cmd_review — replace with direct call. (2) A 4-line skip-list builder loop replaceable with IFS=,; echo. (3) A 13-line priority scope check loop replaceable with a single [[ string match ]]. (4) Verbose startup banner echoing every config value. (5) Nested _count_total_ready and _record_sprint_failure function defs that should be at file scope. (6) Duplicate [[ -s review_output ]] check in post-summary.
steps: Replace 68-line inline auto-review block with a direct call to cmd_review
Replace skip-list builder loop with: local skip_list; skip_list=dollar{!FAILED_IDS[*]}
Replace priority scope check loop with: [[ -z auto_priorities || ' auto_priorities ' == *' item_priority '* ]]
Merge duplicate [[ -s review_output ]] checks into one block
Trim startup banner to essential info only
Verify: auto done --auto-review, auto p1, run --dry-run all work correctly
acceptanceCriteria: No inline review agent invocation in cmd_run — it calls cmd_review
No skip-list builder loop — uses IFS one-liner
No priority scope for-loop — uses string match
No nested function definitions inside cmd_run
auto done --auto-review still runs architecture review after completion
